care.summary: Unique epiphytic fern with antler-like fronds; best mounted on wood or in wire baskets.
care.light: Bright indirect light; tolerates some morning sun, avoid harsh afternoon rays
care.water: Soak mount or pot weekly; let dry slightly between waterings; water shield fronds directly
care.soil: Does not grow in soil — mount on wood board with sphagnum moss or grow in bark/sphagnum mix in wire basket
care.humidity: High (50-70%); thrives in bathrooms or mist regularly
care.fertilizer: Dilute balanced liquid fertilizer monthly in growing season; apply to fronds or moss
care.toxicity: Non-toxic to pets and humans (generally considered safe)
common_issues: Brown frond tips from low humidity or underwatering
Black spots on shield fronds from overwatering or poor air circulation
Scale insects and mealybugs on undersides of fronds
Limp or drooping fronds from insufficient light
tips: Mount on untreated cedar or oak board with sphagnum moss for best growth
Mist fronds daily in dry climates or winter
Do not remove brown shield fronds — they protect the root ball and new growth
Rotate mount periodically for even light distribution
Fertilize by placing banana peel inside shield fronds for natural potassium
